title: `default_sysctls` setting in `containers.conf` not compatible with `podman run network='host'`
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Arch Linux started to include the `containers.conf` as it is which broke `podman run network='host'`.

See: https://bugs.archlinux.org/task/68742

```
default_sysctls = [
 "net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 0",
]
```
of the `containers.conf` leads to a failing execution of `podman run` with `network='host'`:
```bash
[jonny@jonny-arch-pc ~]$ sudo podman run --network='host' docker.io/hello-world
Error: sysctl "net.ipv4.ping_group_range" is not allowed in the hosts network namespace: OCI runtime error
```

Removing of `"net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 0"` fixes the problem:
```bash
[jonny@jonny-arch-pc ~]$ sudo podman run --network='host' docker.io/hello-world

Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.

To generate this message, Docker took the following steps:
 1. The Docker client contacted the Docker daemon.
 2. The Docker daemon pulled the "hello-world" image from the Docker Hub.
    (amd64)
 3. The Docker daemon created a new container from that image which runs the
    executable that produces the output you are currently reading.
 4. The Docker daemon streamed that output to the Docker client, which sent it
    to your terminal.

To try something more ambitious, you can run an Ubuntu container with:
 $ docker run -it ubuntu bash

Share images, automate workflows, and more with a free Docker ID:
 https://hub.docker.com/

For more examples and ideas, visit:
 https://docs.docker.com/get-started/
```

So, I know how to circumvent the problem, until it gets fixed in Arch, I still wonder, and that's why I open this issue:

Are you aware of the implications of including `"net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 0"` in the default `containers.conf` ?

Is this something to be handled by the Linux distributions shipping `podman` or should this rather be handled by you?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Git Commit:   9f6d6ba0b314d86521b66183c9ce48eaa2da1de2
Built:        Tue Nov 24 18:53:00 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
We need to figure out why this is not supported on ArchLinux?

Is this a runc issue?

Error: sysctl "net.ipv4.ping_group_range" is not allowed in the hosts network namespace: OCI runtime error

@giuseppe WDYT?

@polygamma Any chance you could try this with crun?

Perhaps we should ignore these default settings, when the user sets the --net=host.

What version of podman is this happening with?

The code looks like this use case is supposed to be handled, https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/specgen/generate/security.go#L196

This is working fine for me in podman-2.2.0-0.4.rc1.fc33.x86_64

BTW in your homedir, if you setup /etc/containers/containers.conf or $HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf 

with

[containers]
default_sysctls = [
]

It should remove the sysctl.

> What version of podman is this happening with?
> 
> The code looks like this use case is supposed to be handled, https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/specgen/generate/security.go#L196
> 
> This is working fine for me in podman-2.2.0-0.4.rc1.fc33.x86_64

I was using 2.1.1, but I just built 2.2.0-rc2 from source and that indeed fixes the problem

Hi! I did some of the packaging on the podman update when we introduced a containers-common package yesterday.

So, IIUC this can either be fixed by removing the currently present entry for `default_sysctl` during packaging or wait until 2.2.0 is released?
Or does the entry have to be removed anyways? If so I can open a pull request to remove it [here](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/master/pkg/config/containers.conf#L82). :)

Looking at the default file, I also see that a few `default_capabilities` are defined. These also cause issues though ([FS#68743](https://bugs.archlinux.org/task/68743)).

Checking on a fedora32 host I see that both `default_sysctls` and `default_capabilities` are commented by default. I can certainly also do that during packaging, but to serve as a sane default, I think the file should probably just have those commented (upstream), if possible.

The issue is a mismatch between the version of podman and containers.conf, I believe.  We can comment this out upstream, and switch the defaults in the code if you want.  My goal is to push forward the new defaults, but allow distributions to choose their defaults.  I believe the new version is the most secure.  BUT it has to have an updated version of Podman to work correctly.

In Fedora 33 we have the new containers.conf. We did not update fedora 32 with it, yet.  My preference would be to not update Fedora 32.

We are planning on releasing 2.2.0 early next week.  rc2 seems to be working well.

I understand. Maybe it would be most straight forward to provide the internal defaults (commented) in the default containers.conf.

Are there any operating system specific things to take care of to accommodate e.g. for the dropped capabilities with podman 2.2.0 or beyond? Would be great to sync up on that.

I'll keep the defaults commented until podman 2.2.0 is out.

> We need to figure out why this is not supported on ArchLinux?
> 
> Is this a runc issue?
> 
> Error: sysctl "net.ipv4.ping_group_range" is not allowed in the hosts network namespace: OCI runtime error
> 
> @giuseppe WDYT?

this is a correct error from the OCI runtime.  Podman should not set the `net.*` sysctls when using the host network namespace.

I think the commit that fixed it in Podman is: 0d70df119539d818224b0d014602aaad2bd1b95e
Since this is fixed in master and podman-2.2 I am closing this issue.

Seems to be back on Debian 10

```
❯ sudo podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14
Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
Error: OCI runtime error: unable to start container d86bfe05117c2112793b1c374998460eeafdbaef6ea77832b76bd286b1396ec7: sysctl "net.ipv4.ping_group_range" is not allowed in the hosts network namespace
```
Scratch that, after `podman rm mycontainer` and re-creating it, the error resolved itself with podman 3.0.1
